stocks.recent_news: Bank of Maharashtra reported strong Q4 FY26 on April 21: net profit up 35% YoY to Rs 2,014 cr, NII up 18.81% YoY to Rs 3,702 cr, credit growth 22% YoY, deposits up 14% YoY, CASA improved to 52.5%. Declared final dividend of Rs 1.20/share. Stock rallied up to 6.26% to a 52-week high near Rs 80.40; up 21% in the last month and 25% YTD. HDFC Securities retained 'Buy' and raised target to Rs 90 from Rs 80.
stocks.sector_context: Nifty PSU Bank index is up ~15% in April, outperforming Nifty 50 (+9.6%). Q4 FY26 prints from PSU peers (SBI, BOB, Central Bank, Yes Bank) have been broadly healthy with double-digit credit growth and improving asset quality. System credit growth ~13.5% YoY, deposit growth ~11%. Index P/E near 8.7 — still cheap vs private banks.
stocks.peer_comparison: MAHABANK credit growth (22% YoY) is near the top of the PSU pack vs BoB, Canara, Union; asset quality and margins at/above PSU averages. Stock has been one of the best-performing PSU banks over the past month.
red_flags: Deposit growth (14%) lagging loan growth (22%) — funding gap may pressure NIMs if sustained
CASA jump partly driven by seasonal current-account balances, not structural
Stock now at 52-week high after 21% monthly rally — extended on momentum
catalysts: Record date / payout for Rs 1.20 final dividend
Brokerage target upgrades post-results (HDFC Sec already to Rs 90)

stocks.recent_news: PNB Housing Finance Q4 FY26 (reported April 20-21) beat estimates: PAT up 19% YoY to ~Rs 660 cr (14% beat), NII Rs 2,181.5 cr (+7.9% YoY), retail loan book +16% YoY to Rs 86,946 cr, affordable book +~61% YoY to Rs 8,150 cr, GNPA improved to 0.93% from 1.08%. Stock jumped ~11% on April 21 to close near Rs 979.05. MOFSL assigned 'Buy'; stock topped Nifty Smallcap 100 performers.
stocks.sector_context: Housing finance sector benefiting from rate-cut expectations, healthy retail credit demand, and strong affordable-housing push. Peers like LIC Housing, Can Fin, Aavas broadly seeing steady retail AUM growth, but industry-wide pricing/spread pressure remains a theme this cycle.
stocks.peer_comparison: PNBHOUSING's affordable book growth (~61% YoY) is materially above most HFC peers, and asset quality (GNPA 0.93%) compares favorably to large-HFC averages. Post-results rally has made it a smallcap outperformer.
red_flags: MarketsMojo flagged 'underlying concerns' despite the headline profit surge — likely spread/margin compression
NII growth (7.9%) materially slower than loan book growth (16%) — margin pressure visible
Stock up ~11% in a session, near-term profit-booking risk after earnings pop
catalysts: Earnings call / management guidance being digested by brokerages
Monetary policy / rate-cut commentary supportive for HFC sector

stocks.recent_news: Nippon India ETF Gold BeES trading around Rs 125.27 as of April 21; 52-week range Rs 76.36-148.14. MCX gold fell sharply on April 20 (Akshaya Tritiya) — down ~Rs 1,600-5,000, with June futures off 1.12% to Rs 1,52,872/10g; slipped further to Rs 1,53,750/10g on April 21. Silver also corrected ~2% on MCX.
stocks.sector_context: Gold has had a sharp two-day correction after an extended rally driven by geopolitical tensions (Strait of Hormuz) and central-bank buying. RBI kept gold reserves steady while imports fell to a 9-month low; China added gold reserves for the 17th consecutive month. Overall structural demand remains intact but near-term momentum has reversed.
stocks.peer_comparison: Vs peer gold ETFs (SBIGETS, HDFCGOLD, KOTAKGOLD) and SGBs, GOLDBEES is the most-liquid domestic gold ETF (AUM Rs 54,128 cr). Price action tracks physical gold closely; performance in line with category.
red_flags: Two-day sharp correction in domestic gold prices — near-term momentum negative
RBI has paused incremental gold accumulation; India gold imports at 9-month low
Current Rs 125 is ~16% below 52-week high Rs 148 — trend uncertain
catalysts: Any escalation in Middle East / Strait of Hormuz tensions
US Fed rate-path signals (dovish = bullish gold)
Central bank buying data (China, emerging markets)

stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 results not yet announced — board meeting scheduled for May 2026. Street expects revenue Rs 5,000-5,400 cr, PAT Rs 560-630 cr, EBITDA margin 21-24% (12-16% YoY revenue growth). On April 7, CCI approved Torrent Power's acquisition of 100% of Nabha Power from L&T Power Development. Stock trading near Rs 1,580 vs 52-week high Rs 1,940 / low Rs 1,250; 1-year return ~-8%.
stocks.sector_context: Power/utility sector has lagged broader market over the last year — power names have faced headwinds from tariff reviews, elevated valuations post-2024 run-up, and rotation out of capex/PSU themes. Demand fundamentals remain intact with record peak demand and T&D capex, but near-term trading action is subdued.
stocks.peer_comparison: TORNTPOWER lags broader market but offers a clearer growth catalyst via Nabha Power (1,400 MW thermal + potential distribution synergies). Peers JSW Energy, NTPC, Tata Power have had similar range-bound action. Analyst targets Rs 1,880-1,900 imply ~19-20% upside from Rs 1,580.
red_flags: Stock down ~8% over 1 year and ~19% below 52-week high — trend still weak
Nabha Power acquisition adds thermal/coal exposure and integration risk
Power sector broadly out-of-favor vs 2024 run-up
catalysts: Q4 FY26 results in May 2026 (dividend decision on agenda)
Nabha Power acquisition closing and synergy roadmap
Summer peak-demand data supportive of earnings

stocks.recent_news: Nestle India Q4 FY26 (reported April 21) delivered a blowout: net profit up 27% YoY to Rs 1,110.9 cr, revenue up 22.6% YoY to Rs 6,747.8 cr (highest-ever), EBITDA margin 26.3% (well above 22.7-25.1% consensus). Domestic sales Rs 6,445.1 cr (+23.1% YoY) — record. Best quarterly print in 9 years, driven by double-digit volume growth in Maggi, coffee, chocolates. Final dividend Rs 5/share (total FY26 Rs 12). Shares surged 8%+ intraday, closed near Rs 1,393 — a fresh 52-week high.
stocks.sector_context: Nifty FMCG up 2.55% led by Nestle, HUL, Varun Beverages. Sector sees gradual rural recovery (PM-Kisan, good rabi), premiumisation tailwinds; urban demand cautiously stable. HUL Q4 results due April 30; Britannia later. FMCG valuations rich — HUL P/E 33-55, Britannia 56-57.
stocks.peer_comparison: Nestle's 23% domestic revenue growth and double-digit volume growth is materially above HUL (5-6% revenue, 4% volume in Q3) and category averages. Margin expansion to 26.3% is sector-leading. Clearly the standout FY26 Q4 FMCG print so far.
red_flags: Final dividend of Rs 5 below some investor expectations despite record earnings
Valuation very rich post 8% rally at a fresh 52-week high
Large-cap FMCG typically sees muted price action after earnings pop once results are priced in
catalysts: HUL Q4 results April 30 — read-across for sector
Record date for Rs 5 final dividend
